data.title: Illinois to drop collateral lawsuit against Coinbase
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Illinois plans to withdraw its lawsuit against the crypto trading platform Coinbase for staking services, becoming the fourth state to drop related charges after Kentucky, Vermont, and South Carolina. In 2023, Coinbase was jointly sued by ten states for allegedly violating state securities laws through staking services. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) also filed a federal lawsuit against the product, but withdrew the case in February of this year. The states that are still advancing pledge-related lawsuits include: Alabama, California, Maryland, New Jersey, Washington, and Wisconsin. Among them, regulators in New Jersey and Washington said the case is still ongoing, and many other states declined to comment. (CoinDesk)

data.title: JPMorgan Chase raises its forecast for a global recession this year to 60%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bruce Kasman, chief economist at JPMorgan Chase, said that if the tariffs announced by the Trump administration on US trading partners continue, it may push the US and even the global economy into recession in 2025. "The risk of a global recession this year has increased from 40% to 60%," Kasman said in a note on Thursday. He called the tariffs the largest tax increase on American households and businesses since 1968. He also said that the impact of this tax increase is likely to be amplified through retaliation, declining business sentiment and supply chain disruptions. (Jinshi)

data.title: Circle Mints Additional USDC on Solana Blockchain
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Onchain Lens has reported that Circle recently minted 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain. This latest issuance occurred five hours ago. As of now, Circle has minted a total of $11.75 billion USDC on Solana in 2025.

data.title: White House Clarifies Trump's Global Tariff Strategy
data.description: According to Odaily, the Washington Post reports that three informed sources have revealed that White House officials have distributed internal talking points, instructing agents that U.S. President Donald Trump's new global tariff plan should not be seen as a starting point for negotiations. As the world attempts to understand Trump's extensive new import taxes, the administration's internal guidance indicates that advisors should describe the tariffs as a response to a national emergency rather than a foundation for potential new trade talks. Two of the sources noted that, in addition to the talking points, Trump himself has informed advisors that the tariffs are not intended to establish negotiations.

data.title: 5 hours ago Circle minted 250 million USDC on Solana
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Circle minted 250 million USDC on Solana 5 hours ago. So far, they have minted 11.75 billion USDC on Solana in 2025.

data.title: The White House stressed that there is no room for negotiation on tariffs
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the Washington Post, three people familiar with the matter revealed that White House officials distributed internal talking points and told agents that Trump’s new global tariff plan should not be seen as a starting point for negotiations. People familiar with the matter said that as the world tries to understand Trump’s huge new import taxes, the government’s internal instructions stated that advisers should describe tariffs as a response to a national emergency rather than the basis for potential new trade negotiations. Two people familiar with the matter said that in addition to the talking points, Trump himself also told advisers that tariffs are not intended to set up negotiations. (Jin Shi)

data.title: 3 Price Levels to Watch For For Solana Price Recovery
